embed_dtype: float32 (bge-small; NOT the spec dim-768 float8 I/O basis)
candidate_generation: cosine kNN via cuVS CAGRA (bounded pool, TR-1 respected)

topological_scorers.node2vec_skip_reason: training node2vec on 6.9M nodes / 224M edges = multi-billion-token walk corpus + skip-gram training, multi-hour job, out of scope for the cheapest-test remit; CN/AA are training-free

overlap_at_k.note: fraction of each source's top-k reranked candidates that are already out-edges; candidate pool is the cosine top-`pool` for ALL columns, so cosine_only reproduces the 0.110 lower bound and the rest are pure reranks of the identical pool. pool_base_rate is the linked fraction over the whole pool (the reranking ceiling is set by how many true out-edges land in the cosine pool).

verdict: TOPOLOGY ADDS SIGNAL: fused 0.1297 beats cosine 0.11 (Δ+0.0197, 17.9% relative). Graph structure concentrates the true links higher than embedding proximity alone.
gating: compute-bound-ish (dim-384 f32) predictive-signal test, NOT a latency claim (ADR-0017: I/O regime is expected-fail). Real 224M-edge graph, bounded reranking pool.
